description: Homepage & Marketing Pages - Build the high-converting landing page, About page, and Feature Overview page with hero, social proof, feature grid, and CTAs

userStories.title: Features data config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared features configuration file so that feature data is a single source of truth across the homepage grid, feature overview page, and feature detail pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create lib/features.ts exporting an array of feature objects
Each feature has: slug, name, tagline, description, icon (Lucide icon name), href (/features/[slug])
Features include: calendar-scheduling, job-management, fleet-management, online-bookings, customer-communications, invoicing-xero, dashboard-analytics, mobile-app, team-chat
Export typed Feature interface
Feature data reused by homepage grid (this PRD) and feature detail pages (PW-03)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This config is shared across PW-02 (homepage grid, feature overview) and PW-03 (feature detail pages).

userStories.title: Homepage hero section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to immediately understand what VanMan does so that I can decide if it's relevant to my moving business.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage at / with hero section above the fold
Headline: clear value proposition (e.g. 'Run your moving company from one place')
Subheadline: one sentence elaborating the benefit
Primary CTA: 'Start Free Trial' button linking to /signup
Secondary CTA: 'See How It Works' that smooth-scrolls to the how-it-works section
Hero visual: placeholder product screenshot area using Next.js Image component with eager loading (above the fold)
Hero optimized for above-the-fold on desktop and mobile
Hero renders meaningfully without JavaScript (SSR fallback)
Page statically generated (SSG) for maximum performance
Any scroll animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion media query
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: All CTA buttons link to /signup (implemented in PW-04). Homepage encompasses US-002 through US-006 as sections on the same page.

userStories.title: Social proof section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see that other moving companies trust VanMan so that I feel confident signing up.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Trusted by moving companies across NZ, Australia, UK & US' banner text
Customer logos row with placeholder logo images (6 slots)
Stats bar with placeholder numbers: 'X,000+ jobs managed', 'Y companies', 'Z countries'
2-3 customer testimonial cards with name, company, photo placeholder, and quote
Testimonials stored as static data in a lib/testimonials.ts config file with typed Testimonial interface
All below-the-fold images lazy-loaded via Next.js Image component
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Testimonials data is static JSON — no database dependency. Stats are placeholder values to be replaced with real numbers later.

userStories.title: Feature highlights grid
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a quick overview of VanMan's features so that I understand the breadth of the platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Section heading: 'Everything you need to run your moving business'
Responsive grid of feature cards: 3-col desktop, 2-col tablet, 1-col mobile
Cards rendered from the shared lib/features.ts config (US-001)
Each card shows: Lucide icon, title, 1-2 sentence description, 'Learn more →' link to /features/[slug]
All 9 features displayed: calendar-scheduling, job-management, fleet-management, online-bookings, customer-communications, invoicing-xero, dashboard-analytics, mobile-app, team-chat
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. 'Learn more' links go to feature detail pages built in PW-03.

userStories.title: How it works section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to understand the workflow so that I can see how VanMan fits into my daily operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Section with id='how-it-works' (scroll target from hero secondary CTA)
4-step visual flow: 1. Receive bookings, 2. Schedule & dispatch, 3. Track & communicate, 4. Invoice & get paid
Each step: number badge, Lucide icon or illustration placeholder, title, short description
Visual connector between steps (timeline line or arrows)
Responsive: horizontal flow on desktop, vertical stack on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This section is the scroll target from the hero secondary CTA ('See How It Works'). Consider using Framer Motion for subtle step-by-step reveal animation.

userStories.title: Homepage CTA section
userStories.description: As a visitor who has scrolled through the page, I want a clear call to action so that I can sign up.
acceptanceCriteria: Full-width CTA section near bottom of homepage
Heading: 'Ready to simplify your moving business?'
'Start Free Trial' primary button linking to /signup
'Talk to Sales' secondary link to /contact
Subtext: 'No credit card required. Free for 14 days.'
Section uses gradient or dark variant background for visual emphasis
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: About page
userStories.description: As a visitor who wants to learn more about the company, I want an About page so that I can understand who's behind VanMan.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /about
Company story section: origin story — built by a real removalist company (VanMan NZ)
Mission statement: 'We're building the tools we wished we had when running our own moving company'
'Built by movers, for movers' messaging for authenticity and domain expertise
Team section with placeholder cards (photo, name, role)
CTA at bottom to start free trial linking to /signup
Page statically generated (SSG)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: About page establishes trust and credibility. Team section uses placeholder data initially — real team info to be provided. Content tone: authentic, approachable, not corporate.

userStories.title: Feature overview page
userStories.description: As a visitor who wants a comprehensive view, I want a single page listing all features so that I can evaluate VanMan's full capability.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /features

Feature categories with anchor links: Scheduling & Calendar, Job Management, Fleet Management, Booking & Lead Capture, Communications, Invoicing & Accounting, Analytics & Reporting, Mobile App, Team Chat
Each category: heading, 3-4 bullet points describing capabilities, screenshot/illustration placeholder, 'Learn more →' link to /features/[slug] detail page
Sticky sidebar or top nav on desktop for jumping between category sections
Page statically generated (SSG)
Data driven from the shared lib/features.ts config (US-001)
All images use Next.js Image component with lazy loading below the fold
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This page links to individual feature pages built in PW-03. Sticky sidebar aids navigation for the long-scroll page.
